Title: Manipulation of artificial light environment improves plant biomass and fruit nutritional quality in tomato

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; sugar profiling; phenotype comparison under light treatments
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described by authors as volatile-related gene and highlighted as SlAADC1a-dependent in improved volatile metabolism; no direct genetic perturbation of SlAADC1a was performed in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to improve tomato aroma/volatile quality under controlled lighting; could be combined with sugar and carotenoid quality improvement strategies.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with improved volatile metabolism and fruit quality under R1B0.8 light.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile compounds contribute to tomato aroma and eating quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAADC1a expression was significantly induced by R1B0.8 treatment in tomato fruit. 2. The paper highlights improved volatile metabolism in an SlAADC1a-dependent manner. 3. This gene is implicated in light-enhanced fruit flavor quality.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; sugar and quality trait measurements under light treatments
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as flavor-related gene GORKY (glycoalkaloid transporter); implicated in fruit quality response to light but metabolite transport substrate details were not experimentally resolved in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/target for improving tomato flavor-related quality traits in greenhouse lighting programs.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with improved flavor-related quality under R1B0.8 light.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavor-related specialized metabolites affect fruit sensory quality.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlGORKY expression increased under R1B0.8 treatment. 2. The induction coincided with improved fruit quality traits. 3. SlGORKY is implicated in light-responsive flavor-related metabolism in tomato fruit.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance tomato fruit carotenoid and lycopene accumulation under controlled-environment production; can be combined with light management or stacked with downstream carotenoid-pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid and lycopene accumulation in tomato fruit under R1B0.8 light-associated upregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl diphosphate to form phytoene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene expression was assessed by qRT-PCR; causality is supported at expression-metabolite association level under light treatment, not by transgenic manipulation in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Carotenoids contribute to fruit nutritional quality and ripening-associated coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPSY1 transcript levels were significantly induced by R1B0.8 light in tomato fruits. 2. This induction coincided with increased total carotenoid and lycopene accumulation. 3. SlPSY1 is implicated as a key contributor to light-enhanced fruit nutritional quality.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of carotenoids and lycopene under light treatments

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate engineering target to reinforce carotenoid flux together with PSY1 under optimized red/blue light regimes in greenhouse tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced carotenoid and lycopene accumulation under R1B0.8 light through increased expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces desaturation into phytoene to generate downstream carotenoid intermediates toward lycopene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in this study is expression association with fruit carotenoid phenotype under light manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fruit nutritional quality and red pigmentation via carotenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlPDS expression increased under R1B0.8. 2. Increased expression paralleled higher carotenoid and lycopene contents. 3. SlPDS likely contributes to enhanced carotenoid biosynthetic flux under optimized artificial light.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for tuning ripening speed and texture in tomato fruit production; should be balanced against shelf-life loss when combined with light treatments that accelerate ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fruit ripening and softening under R1B0.8-associated upregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC, the direct precursor of ethylene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study links increased SlACS2 expression with reduced fruit firmness under light treatment; ethylene itself was not directly quantified here.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce fruit firmness and shelf life due to faster ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ethylene regulates climacteric fruit ripening and softening.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlACS2 was strongly upregulated by R1B0.8 in fruits. 2. This was associated with accelerated softening and ripening. 3. SlACS2 is implicated in light-responsive ethylene-mediated fruit quality changes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; firmness measurement under light treatments

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for fine-tuning ripening completion in protected cultivation; manipulation should consider softening and shelf-life tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Red:blue mixed light R1B0.8; postharvest fruit light treatment; 12 h photoperiod for 10 d
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with accelerated fruit ripening and decreased firmness under R1B0.8 light.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in fruits under R1B0.8 light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ACC to produce ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is based on expression changes correlating with ripening phenotype under light treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May accelerate softening and shorten shelf life.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ethylene promotes climacteric ripening and fruit softening.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlACO2 transcript abundance increased under R1B0.8. 2. This increase paralleled reduced fruit firmness and faster ripening. 3. SlACO2 likely contributes to light-enhanced ethylene production capacity in fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit; induced after 10 d R1B0.8 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; firmness assay under light treatments
